paper_title: Pollen Analysis and Age Model of ODP Hole 642B

methods.description: This method describes the preparation of sediment samples for palynological analysis to study pollen assemblages and their preservation. It includes sieving, chemical treatment, and microscopic analysis to identify and count pollen grains.

steps.description: A total of 70 samples were taken from ODP Hole 642B, of which 68 samples were situated between 69.90 and 66.95 m below sea floor (m b.s.f.).
steps.searchable_summary: collect sediment samples from specific depths

steps.description: Samples were pre-sieved through a 63 µm mesh to retain foraminifera for oxygen isotope analysis.
Samples were prepared for palynological analysis using standard palynological techniques.
One Lycopodium clavatum spore tablet was added to each sample to allow pollen concentration calculations.
steps.searchable_summary: add Lycopodium clavatum spore tablet to samples

steps.description: The samples were treated with HCl and HF to remove carbonates and silicates.
steps.searchable_summary: treat samples with HCl and HF

steps.description: The sediment was back-sieved through a 10 µm screen and the residue was mounted on glass slides with glycerol-gelatine jelly.
steps.searchable_summary: back-sieve sediment and mount on slides

steps.description: Microscopic analysis was carried out using a Leica Microscope at magnifications of 400× and 1000×.
Pollen diagrams were generated with the software Tilia.
Stratigraphically constrained cluster analysis was performed to delimit pollen zones.
steps.searchable_summary: perform cluster analysis to define pollen zones

steps.description: Pollen percentages were calculated on the total pollen sum excluding Pinus pollen.
Rarefaction was applied to estimate the number of taxa at a constant count of grains.
steps.searchable_summary: apply rarefaction to estimate taxa number

steps.description: Diversity was assessed using the Shannon index.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate Shannon index for diversity assessment

methods.description: This method outlines the construction of an age model for ODP Hole 642B using magnetic stratigraphy and correlation of benthic oxygen isotopes to a global stack.

steps.description: The Pliocene age model for ODP Hole 642B is based on the magnetic stratigraphy updated to the Astronomically Tuned Neogene Time Scale (ATNTS) 2012.
steps.searchable_summary: construct age model using magnetic stratigraphy and ATNTS 2012

steps.description: Correlation of the benthic oxygen isotopes to the global LR04 benthic δ 18 O stack.
steps.searchable_summary: correlate benthic oxygen isotopes to LR04 stack

steps.description: The investigated samples have an age range of 3.60 to 3.14 Ma.
The upper part of the sequence covers the central part of interglacial MIS KM5c (3.205 Ma).
The modern sample presumably covers less than 2000 years, while the subsurface sample at 3 cm depth possibly reveals a maximum age of 6000 years.
steps.searchable_summary: estimate age of modern and subsurface samples
